title: Geographical Location
paragraphs: Nepalgunj Sub-Metropolitan City is located in Banke District, one of the 77 districts within the 7 provinces of Nepal, covering a total area of 85.94 sq. km. Geographically, this sub-metropolis is spread from 81° 17' 24" to 82° 48' 00" East longitude and from 27° 30' 36" North to 28° 12' 00" North latitude. The sub-metropolis is bounded by Duduwa Rural Municipality to the east, Janaki Rural Municipality to the west, Janaki Rural Municipality and Kohalpur Municipality to the north, and Duduwa Rural Municipality and India to the south. Most of the area here falls under the flat Terai, with a predominance of extremely fertile soil mixed with sand and silt. Due to the tropical climate, it is very cold in the months of Poush and Magh, and very hot in Baisakh, Jestha, and Ashadh. Known as one of the hottest cities in Nepal, the minimum temperature here reaches from 4.5 degrees Celsius to a maximum temperature of 46 degrees Celsius, while the average annual rainfall is 1445.58 milliliters.
